---
title: "Ferrum Architecture: A GA4GH-Native Genomics Platform"
subtitle: "Synaptic Four Technical Report SF-TR-2026-001"

author:
  - name: Synaptic Four
    affiliation: Synaptic Four
    email: contact@synapticfour.com
    url: https://synapticfour.com

date: "2026-06-09"
date-format: "D MMMM YYYY"

# Report metadata (custom fields — used by website templates)
version: "1.0.1"
report-id: SF-TR-2026-001
report-category: architecture
series: "Synaptic Four Technical Reports"

keywords:
  - GA4GH
  - genomics
  - Ferrum
  - DRS
  - WES
  - TES
  - TRS
  - htsget
  - Crypt4GH
  - HelixTest
  - on-premises

abstract: |
  Ferrum implements a broad set of Global Alliance for Genomics and Health (GA4GH)
  APIs in one composable, on-premises-first runtime. This technical report documents
  motivation, system architecture, Crypt4GH integration, multi-engine workflow
  execution, validation with the independent HelixTest conformance suite, and
  reproducible benchmarks via the Apache-2.0 Ferrum GA4GH Demo. It also explains
  how Synaptic Four uses AI-assisted engineering transparently — as acceleration,
  not a substitute for engineering judgement.

license: "CC BY 4.0"

# DOI is assigned on Zenodo upload; set here once available
doi: "10.5281/zenodo.20612210"
zenodo: "https://zenodo.org/records/20612210"
github-release: "https://github.com/SynapticFour/Ferrum/releases"

citation:
  type: report
  publisher: Synaptic Four
  doi: "10.5281/zenodo.20612210"
  url: "https://zenodo.org/records/20612210"
  issued: 2026-06-09

bibliography: references.bib
csl: ../../csl/ieee.csl   # IEEE numeric style; adjust path to repo root

format:

  # ── HTML (website / GitHub Pages) ────────────────────────────────────
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 3
    theme: cosmo
    highlight-style: github
    code-overflow: wrap
    fig-align: center
    cap-location: bottom
    crossref:
      fig-title: "Figure"
      tbl-title: "Table"
    html-math-method: katex
    link-external-icon: true
    link-external-newwindow: true

  # ── PDF (Zenodo archival + download) ─────────────────────────────────
  pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    number-sections: true
    number-depth: 3
    documentclass: article
    papersize: a4
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry:
      - top=25mm
      - bottom=25mm
      - left=25mm
      - right=25mm
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: "NavyBlue"
    urlcolor: "NavyBlue"
    citecolor: "NavyBlue"
    fig-align: center
    fig-width: 5.8
    fig-height: 5.0
    cap-location: bottom
    crossref:
      fig-title: "Figure"
      tbl-title: "Table"
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{booktabs}
        \usepackage{longtable}
        \usepackage{array}
        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \usepackage{lastpage}
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \definecolor{ferrumnavy}{HTML}{1B2D5B}
        \definecolor{ferrumteal}{HTML}{2E8B8B}
        \pagestyle{fancy}
        \fancyhf{}
        \fancyhead[L]{\small\color{gray}Synaptic Four · SF-TR-2026-001}
        \fancyhead[R]{\small\color{ferrumteal}\textbf{TECHNICAL REPORT}}
        \fancyfoot[L]{\small\color{gray}DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.20612210 · CC BY 4.0}
        \fancyfoot[R]{\small\color{gray}Page \thepage\ of \pageref{LastPage}}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0.4pt}
        \renewcommand{\footrulewidth}{0.4pt}
        \setlength{\headheight}{14pt}
---

# Executive Summary {.unnumbered}

Ferrum is an integrated, on-premises-first runtime that composes a broad set of
GA4GH APIs — TRS, DRS, WES, TES, htsget, Beacon, Passports, and Crypt4GH —
behind a unified Rust gateway with shared authentication. Synaptic Four built
Ferrum because cross-service, reproducible GA4GH stacks are rare in production
despite strong individual component implementations.

This report is intended for data architects, genomics platform engineers, and
standards implementers evaluating integrated GA4GH infrastructure. Principal
contributions include:

1. A documented architecture for selective deployment of GA4GH services.
2. Crypt4GH integration at the DRS layer with O(1) header re-wrapping.
3. Multi-engine WES execution via Cromwell, Nextflow, CWL, and Snakemake.
4. Continuous cross-service validation through HelixTest.
5. Reproducible benchmark artefacts from the Ferrum GA4GH Demo.

Benchmark scope is intentionally modest — small synthetic-style genomic slices
with stated repetition counts. Results are a technical conformance and
performance signal, not official GA4GH certification.

---

# Introduction

The Global Alliance for Genomics and Health (GA4GH) defines interoperable APIs
for genomic data discovery, access, and compute [@ga4gh2024framework]. Research
organisations, clinical laboratories, and national infrastructures increasingly
require implementations that exercise multiple standards together in real
pipelines — not isolated API endpoints.

This report documents the Ferrum platform architecture, implementation choices,
validation methodology, and deployment patterns. It supersedes the shorter web
overview at [synapticfour.com/ferrum-white-paper](https://synapticfour.com/ferrum-white-paper)
as the canonical technical source for Ferrum architecture (identifier SF-TR-2026-001).

**Audience:** Bioinformaticians, research software engineers, data architects,
infrastructure teams, and GA4GH implementers.

**Scope:** Architecture, integration patterns, validation, and benchmark
methodology. Operational runbooks for specific customer deployments are out of
scope.

# Background

## GA4GH Service Landscape

GA4GH specifies APIs that together cover the full genomic data lifecycle — from
object storage and access through workflow execution to variant discovery and
authorisation. @tbl-ga4gh-apis summarises the services addressed in Ferrum.

| API | Purpose |
|:---|:---|
| **TRS** | Tool Registry Service — workflow and tool descriptors |
| **DRS** | Data Repository Service — object storage and access |
| **WES** | Workflow Execution Service — workflow run management |
| **TES** | Task Execution Service — compute task dispatch |
| **htsget** | Partial read retrieval from BAM/CRAM/VCF sequencing files |
| **Beacon** | Variant existence queries |
| **Passports** | Authorisation and data access policies (OIDC-based) |
| **Crypt4GH** | Encrypted genomics data exchange; O(1) header re-wrapping [@crypt4gh2020] |

: GA4GH APIs implemented in Ferrum. {#tbl-ga4gh-apis tbl-colwidths="[18,82]"}

Production systems often implement subsets. DRS object access patterns follow
the GA4GH DRS specification v1 [@drs2021v1]. Cross-service stacks with unified
authentication, shared metadata, and continuous conformance testing remain
uncommon.

## Synaptic Four Context

Synaptic Four is a Stuttgart-based consultancy specialising in bioinformatics,
federated data infrastructure, and GA4GH-oriented platform engineering. Ferrum
serves as both a deployable platform and a public demonstration of engineering
practice: precise, documented, and test-driven.

# Problem Statement

Research and clinical genomics teams need:

1. **Integrated GA4GH services** that work together in end-to-end pipelines
   (TRS → DRS → WES → TES).
2. **On-premises-first deployment** with selective service activation — no
   mandatory cloud dependency.
3. **Encryption at rest and per-requester delivery** via Crypt4GH
   [@crypt4gh2020] without breaking DRS access patterns [@drs2021v1].
4. **Reproducible validation** that any operator can run independently
   [@helixtest2026].
5. **Honest performance claims** with documented dataset scope and limitations,
   aligned with FAIR data practice [@wilkinson2016fair].

Existing component implementations — TESK, Funnel, cwl-WES, WESkit, GA4GH
Starter Kit pieces, Galaxy integrations — address individual standards well. The
gap is a focused integration point combining TRS + DRS + WES + TES + htsget +
Beacon + Passports + Crypt4GH in one runtime with a unified gateway and
continuous cross-service tests.

# Related Work

Strong open-source implementations exist across the GA4GH ecosystem:

- **TESK / Funnel** — TES implementations for Kubernetes and HPC batch systems.
- **cwl-WES / WESkit** — WES backends for CWL workflows.
- **GA4GH Starter Kit** — Reference implementations for individual APIs.
- **Galaxy** — Workflow platform with broad GA4GH integrations.

Ferrum does not replace these projects. It targets organisations needing a
composable, on-premises-first runtime that exercises the standards together.
Ferrum Lab Kit (BUSL-1.1) provides an opinionated on-ramp for laboratories,
ELIXIR node candidates, and GDI-style footprints.

Independent validation is provided by **HelixTest** (Apache-2.0)
[@helixtest2026], maintained by Synaptic Four and reusable against any
GA4GH-compatible platform.

# System Architecture

## High-Level Design

Services are composed behind a single **ferrum-gateway** (Rust, async Tokio)
[@ferrum2026]. Metadata resides in PostgreSQL; objects in S3-compatible storage
(MinIO, cloud S3, POSIX, OpenDAL-backed backends). The gateway routes requests
to enabled GA4GH services; operators run only the services required for their
footprint.

@fig-dataplane illustrates the logical data-plane flow in the Ferrum GA4GH Demo
stack.

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-dataplane
%%| fig-cap: "Ferrum GA4GH Demo data plane — logical flow. Arrows represent the direction of API calls. Numbers correspond to the walk-through in @sec-dataplane-walkthrough."
%%| fig-width: 5.8
%%| fig-height: 5.0
flowchart TB
  subgraph Client["Client"]
    R["demo/run.sh"]
  end

  subgraph FerrumStack["Ferrum (Docker Compose)"]
    direction TB
    G["ferrum-gateway\n(Rust · async)"]

    subgraph Services["GA4GH Services"]
      direction LR
      DRS["DRS\n/ingest · /objects · /stream"]
      TRS["TRS\n/tools"]
      WES["WES\n/runs"]
      TES["TES\n/tasks"]
    end

    subgraph Storage["Storage"]
      direction LR
      PG[("PostgreSQL\nmetadata")]
      S3[("MinIO\nobject store")]
    end
  end

  subgraph Exec["TES Execution"]
    direction LR
    C["Cromwell · Nextflow\nCWL · Snakemake"]
    GK["GATK container\n(docker.sock)"]
  end

  R -->|"① multipart ingest"| G
  G --> DRS
  G --> TRS
  G --> WES
  G --> TES

  R -->|"② Dockstore TRS lookup"| TRS
  R -->|"③ POST /runs"| WES
  WES -->|"④ POST /tasks"| TES
  TES -->|"⑤ Docker API"| C
  C -->|"⑥ docker.sock"| GK

  DRS --- PG
  DRS --- S3
```

## Gateway and Service Composition

The gateway authenticates every incoming request and routes it to the
appropriate GA4GH service. Deployment is **selective**: operators enable only
the services their footprint requires via Docker Compose profiles and feature
flags. This limits attack surface and resource consumption.

## Data Plane Walk-Through {#sec-dataplane-walkthrough}

A full demonstration run proceeds in eight steps:

1. **Ingest** — `POST /ga4gh/drs/v1/ingest/file` submits objects as multipart
   form data. The DRS service stores bytes in the S3-compatible backend and
   writes metadata to PostgreSQL.
2. **Gateway routing** — ferrum-gateway authenticates the request and dispatches
   to the target service.
3. **Object access** — `GET /ga4gh/drs/v1/objects/{id}/stream` delivers bytes;
   Crypt4GH profiles optionally encrypt at rest and re-wrap headers
   per-requester.
4. **Tool resolution** — TRS resolves workflow and tool descriptors, including
   Dockstore `ga4gh/trs/v2` integration.
5. **Workflow execution** — WES receives `POST /runs` and submits a workflow
   run.
6. **Task dispatch** — TES dispatches compute tasks to the configured engine
   (Cromwell, Nextflow, CWL, Snakemake).
7. **Container execution** — The engine invokes GATK via the Docker socket.
8. **Result storage** — Outputs are written to the object store; benchmark
   artefacts are emitted to `results/`.

## Crypt4GH Integration

Crypt4GH is integrated at the DRS layer with three operational modes:

- **At-rest encryption** — Objects ingested with `encrypt=true` are stored
  encrypted; ferrum-gateway decrypts transparently on `/stream`.
- **O(1) header re-wrapping** — Per-requester delivery via
  `X-Crypt4GH-Public-Key` rewrites only the 64-byte Crypt4GH header; the
  encrypted body is never re-processed.
- **Passport-gated re-wrap** — Re-wrap operations require Passport/OIDC-style
  authentication in stock Ferrum, preventing unauthorised key substitution.

# Implementation

## Technology Stack

@tbl-tech-stack lists the primary technologies used in Ferrum.

| Component | Technology |
|:---|:---|
| Gateway | Rust (async Tokio) |
| Metadata store | PostgreSQL |
| Object storage | MinIO · AWS S3 · POSIX · OpenDAL backends |
| Workflow engines | Cromwell (WDL) · Nextflow · CWL · Snakemake |
| HPC schedulers | SLURM · LSF (on-premises) |
| Authentication | Keycloak / OIDC (GA4GH Passports) |

: Ferrum technology stack. {#tbl-tech-stack tbl-colwidths="[28,72]"}

## WES → TES Execution

WES backends route workflow runs to TES with engine-specific launch
configurations:

- **WDL** — Cromwell via TES Docker backend with host-mounted work directories.
- **Nextflow** — `nextflow run` with Docker enabled; NF 24+ configuration
  patterns.
- **CWL / Snakemake** — Routed via TES with appropriate container backends.

Environment variables control work directory paths, Docker socket mounting, and
executor images. Full reference:
[Ferrum TES Docker Backend documentation](https://github.com/SynapticFour/Ferrum/blob/main/docs/TES-DOCKER-BACKEND.md).

## Ferrum GA4GH Demo

The [Ferrum GA4GH Demo](https://github.com/SynapticFour/Ferrum-GA4GH-Demo)
(Apache-2.0) [@ferrum_demo2026] provides a reproducible end-to-end stack:

- **Dataset** — GIAB-style synthetic subset; GRCh37 chr22:1700–2300 window for
  controlled, fast iteration.
- **DRS micro-benchmarks** — `scripts/drs_micro_benchmark.py`; object ingest
  and `/stream` latency.
- **Macro comparison** — plain vs Crypt4GH-at-rest ingest, full pipeline
  end-to-end.
- **Scientific validation** — hap.py precision / recall / F1 metrics confirming
  genomic equivalence.
- **Machine-readable artefacts** — `benchmark.json`, `drs_micro.json`,
  `engine_compare.json` in `results/`.

# Deployment

## Selective Service Deployment

Operators activate only the GA4GH services their use case requires. Ferrum Lab
Kit (BUSL-1.1) provides an opinionated Docker Compose stack designed as an
on-ramp for:

- Research and clinical laboratories.
- ELIXIR node candidates.
- GDI-style national infrastructure footprints.

## Resource Planning

@tbl-resources gives indicative resource requirements based on the demo stack.

| Profile | RAM | Disk | Notes |
|:---|:---|:---|:---|
| Demo subset | 8–12 GB | ~5–15 GB | Synthetic GIAB-style slice |
| Macro + Crypt4GH | 8–12 GB | + encrypted objects | ~2× pipeline time vs plain |
| Production WGS | 32–64 GB+ | 200 GB–1 TB+ | Not demonstrated; estimate only |

: Demo reference resource planning. {#tbl-resources}

## Reproducibility

All demo repositories pin software versions, document environment variables
exhaustively, and emit machine-readable benchmark artefacts alongside each run.
`./run` scripts manage the full Docker Compose lifecycle; `--no-reset` enables
faster iteration by skipping volume teardown between runs.

# Security Considerations

- **Authentication** — Passports/OIDC via Keycloak; ferrum-gateway enforces
  authentication on all sensitive operations including Crypt4GH re-wrap.
- **Encryption** — Crypt4GH at-rest and in-transit profiles; keys are managed
  outside the gateway, per deployment.
- **Least privilege** — Selective service deployment minimises the exposed API
  surface area.
- **Audit** — HelixTest exercises authentication flows in CI; production audit
  logging is deployment-specific and out of scope here.

::: {.callout-important}
This report does not constitute a formal security audit. Deployment-specific
threat models should be developed for any regulated or clinical environment.
:::

# Performance Considerations

Benchmarks are drawn from the Ferrum GA4GH Demo run of 2026-05-08 using the WDL
engine (Cromwell). All metrics reflect the synthetic GIAB-style chr22 subset
described in @sec-demo.

| Metric | Value |
|:---|:---|
| Pipeline wall time — WDL / Cromwell | 199 s |
| Pipeline wall time — Nextflow | 30 s (separate pass) |
| DRS `/stream` plain — median latency | ~4.5 ms (n = 3) |
| hap.py precision / recall / F1 | 1.0 / 1.0 / 1.0 |
| Input dataset | Synthetic subset · chr22:1700–2300 (GRCh37) |
| Total ingest size | ~5.4 KiB (six DRS objects) |

: Benchmark results — Ferrum GA4GH Demo, 2026-05-08, WDL engine. {#tbl-benchmarks}

::: {.callout-warning}
**Limitations.** Medians based on n = 3 repetitions over small synthetic slices.
Cromwell vs Nextflow times are full end-to-end per pass (ingest + DRS micro-bench
+ WES + hap.py), not GATK-only execution. Results must not be extrapolated to
whole-genome production workloads without independent measurement at scale.
:::

# Limitations

1. **Benchmark scope** — Demo uses small synthetic genomic slices, not
   production-scale WGS. Larger GIAB subsets are planned (see @sec-future).
2. **Certification** — HelixTest results are a technical conformance signal, not
   official GA4GH certification.
3. **Licensing** — Ferrum core is BUSL-1.1; HelixTest and Ferrum GA4GH Demo are
   Apache-2.0. Licensing terms vary by repository; consult each repository's
   `LICENSE` file.
4. **Cloud-native features** — Ferrum prioritises on-premises deployment;
   managed-cloud patterns are not the primary focus.
5. **Single-vendor auth** — Stock demo uses Keycloak; other IdP integrations are
   deployment-specific.

# Future Work {#sec-future}

- Expand benchmark coverage to larger GIAB subsets with published dataset
  profiles and extended repetition counts.
- Upstream gateway patches from the demo overlay (TES backend selection, work
  directory routing).
- Additional WES engine profiles and HPC scheduler integrations (PBS, OAR).
- Federation patterns with Mycelium data infrastructure (see planned
  SF-TR-2026-002).
- Formal security audit for regulated clinical deployment footprints.

# Conclusion

A small team can deliver a unified GA4GH-oriented runtime when standards, tests,
and honest scope are treated as first-class engineering concerns. Ferrum combines
broad API coverage, selective deployment, Crypt4GH integration, and multi-engine
execution in one composable runtime. HelixTest and the Ferrum GA4GH Demo make
architectural claims independently verifiable.

Synaptic Four welcomes collaboration, deployment partnerships, and research
contact at [contact@synapticfour.com](mailto:contact@synapticfour.com).

# Acknowledgements {.unnumbered}

The GA4GH community and contributors to TESK, Funnel, cwl-WES, WESkit, the
GA4GH Starter Kit, Galaxy, and related projects. GIAB reference materials for
benchmark subsets. Open-source maintainers of Rust, PostgreSQL, MinIO, Cromwell,
Nextflow, and GATK.

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::

# Appendices {.unnumbered}

## Appendix A — Related Repositories {.unnumbered}

| Repository | License | Role |
|:---|:---|:---|
| [Ferrum](https://github.com/SynapticFour/Ferrum) | BUSL-1.1 | Core GA4GH runtime (gateway + services) |
| [Ferrum GA4GH Demo](https://github.com/SynapticFour/Ferrum-GA4GH-Demo) | Apache-2.0 | Reproducible end-to-end benchmark stack |
| [HelixTest](https://github.com/SynapticFour/HelixTest) | Apache-2.0 | Independent GA4GH conformance test suite |
| [Ferrum Lab Kit](https://github.com/SynapticFour/Ferrum-Lab-Kit) | BUSL-1.1 | Opinionated lab / ELIXIR node on-ramp |

: Related repositories and licenses. {#tbl-repos}

## Appendix B — Glossary {.unnumbered}

| Term | Definition |
|:---|:---|
| DRS | Data Repository Service (GA4GH v1) |
| WES | Workflow Execution Service (GA4GH v1) |
| TES | Task Execution Service (GA4GH v1) |
| TRS | Tool Registry Service (GA4GH v2) |
| htsget | HTTP-based protocol for partial retrieval from BAM/CRAM/VCF files |
| Crypt4GH | GA4GH encrypted genomics file format; supports O(1) header re-wrapping |
| HelixTest | Independent GA4GH conformance test suite (Apache-2.0, Synaptic Four) |
| GIAB | Genome in a Bottle — NIST reference material used for benchmark subsets |
| BUSL-1.1 | Business Source License 1.1 — converts to Apache-2.0 after change date |
| OIDC | OpenID Connect — identity layer used for GA4GH Passports |
| OpenDAL | Apache OpenDAL — unified data access layer supporting S3, POSIX, and more |

: Glossary of terms and abbreviations. {#tbl-glossary}

## Appendix C — Change Log {.unnumbered}

| Version | Date | Summary |
|:---|:---|:---|
| 1.0.0 | 2026-06-09 | Initial release; migrated from Ferrum white paper web content |
| 1.0.1 | 2026-06-09 | PDF layout fixes; inline citations; PDF-safe architecture figure |

: Document change log. {#tbl-changelog}

## Appendix D — Relationship to Web White Paper {.unnumbered}

The Ferrum white paper at
[synapticfour.com/publications](https://synapticfour.com/publications) provides
a shorter overview with PDF downloads (EN/DE). This technical report
(SF-TR-2026-001) is the canonical, version-controlled source for Ferrum
architecture. Future revisions will be released via GitHub tags and archived on
Zenodo under the concept DOI `10.5281/zenodo.20612210`, which always resolves to
the latest version.
